events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes tile installed on a single-layer subfloor with deflection. This is a common failure mode in residential flooring. Tile requires a stiff substrate; single-layer subfloor (typically 5/8" or 3/4" plywood) without additional underlayment or backer board is insufficient and will deflect under load, causing tile cracking and grout failure.

TCNA (Tile Council of North America) handbook specifies minimum subfloor requirements for tile installations - typically requires double-layer wood subfloor or appropriate backer board/membrane over single-layer subfloor. The deflection criteria (L/360 for ceramic, L/720 for stone) must be met.

The fault is: tile installed over single-layer subfloor without proper underlayment/backer board, leading to deflection and likely tile/grout cracking.

Decision: fail (non-compliant with TCNA and manufacturer instructions)
Risk: high (tile will crack, potential trip hazard from loose tiles)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded (if already showing signs), possibly failed
Work stage: in-service
Percent complete: 100 (it's in service)

Let me construct the JSON.

    "Verify floor joist spacing and span meet L/360 deflection limit for ceramic / L/720 for stone before re-installing tile",
    "Re-install tile per manufacturer instructions and TCNA handbook detail"
  ],
  "rationale": "Single-layer subfloor does not provide the stiffness required by TCNA for ceramic tile. Deflection under load causes the bond layer and tile to crack. The installation is non-compliant and will continue to degrade in service.",

    "TCNA Handbook — Subfloor deflection criteria L/360 ceramic / L/720 stone",
    "Manufacturer tile and mortar installation instructions — substrate requirements"
  ]
